message:
fix(io): suppress spurious waker dispatch on poll error and optimize epoll modify

I/O driver (io_driver.rs):
- Added restore_events_only() that clears the events buffer without
  dispatching any wakers.  Previously, when the reactor poll() returned
  an error, the error path called restore_and_dispatch with a no-op
  callback — but the backend may have left stale events in the scratch
  buffer from a prior successful poll.  If those events matched valid
  tokens, the dispatch loop would wake tasks based on fabricated
  readiness, causing spurious I/O attempts.  Now the error path calls
  restore_events_only() which clears events first.
- Added PollErrorWithEventReactor test mock that injects a stale event
  into the events buffer AND returns Err from poll().
- Added test: io_driver_handle_turn_with_poll_error_does_not_dispatch —
  verifies that after a poll error with a stale event present, the
  registered waker is NOT fired, stats show zero dispatches, and the
  waker registration is preserved for the next successful poll.

Epoll reactor (epoll.rs):
- Refactored modify() to use BTreeMap's Entry API instead of separate
  get/get_mut/remove calls.  The previous flow did: get(&token) to read
  raw_fd, then on success get_mut(&token) to update interest, or on
  error remove(&token) — three separate O(log n) lookups on the same
  key.  Now a single entry() call is reused for read, update, and
  removal, saving 1-2 tree traversals per modify on the hot path.
- Used struct destructuring (let ReactorState { tokens, fds } = &mut
  *state) to split borrows, allowing the entry handle on tokens and
  fds.remove() to coexist without borrow conflicts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
